rows.notes: Clear, centered year at top of page. Very legible handwriting and no amounts on this line.

rows.description: Computus Rectoria a 2d die Novembris A.D. 1859
rows.notes: First line of the Latin header; text is clear and spans the top. No monetary amounts.
Second line of the Latin header; clear and legible. No amounts.
Horizontal rule / separator under the header. No text or monetary amounts on this visual line.
Section heading ('Recep ta' / 'Recepta') centered beneath the rule. Handwriting slightly stylised but unambiguous as a header.
Amount '10' clearly written in the pounds column when aligned using the total (row 26) as the vertical guide. Shillings/pence columns blank.
Pounds '30' clearly visible in the pounds column after projection of column boundaries from the total row. Description is abbreviated in the original; reading confident.

Smaller amount aligned to columns reads as '1 8 6' (1 pound, 8 shillings, 6 pence). The leading '1' in the pounds column is faint and slightly ambiguous due to ink/light stroke; chosen based on alignment with the vertical guides from row 26.
Large sum '1308 6 7' clearly aligned in the pounds/shillings/pence columns. Column boundaries confirmed against row 26; digits are crisp.
Second large sum below Colby line reads '1986 0 7'. Clear alignment and digits; '0' shillings is evident in the middle column.
Amount '77 13 4' is well aligned and legible. All sub-values are within valid ranges.

rows.description: Ditto a Mr. Jones consols recd Mint '58
rows.notes: Description is abbreviated and slightly hard to parse ('Do.' / 'consols'); amount '17 2 9' is readable though some letters in the description are faint.
Amount '154 13 9' clear in the columns. The word 'Ant.' and 'Closes' in description are slightly abbreviated but unambiguous in context.

rows.description: late Building Fund - not carried to last Acct
rows.notes: Reads as '47 1'. Pence column appears blank for this line (no pence written). Alignment checked against row 26. Some letters in description are faint but meaning clear.
Single '10' in the pounds column is clear. Description legible.
Amount '11 8 6' well aligned and legible. Description readable with normal abbreviation/spacing.
Large sum '738 11 8' very clear in columns; alignment unambiguous using the total row guide.
Reads as '332 10' (pence column blank). The word 'Colls' (colleges/collections) is abbreviated; amount digits are distinct and within expected range.

rows.description: Overplus Room Rent Xmas 1858 & Do 1859 Rents
rows.notes: Amount appears as '7 11' (pence blank). The description is cramped and the year numerals are slightly faint; used column alignment from row 26 to place amounts.

rows.description: Repayment of money borrowed by Kater from Caution Fund - Interest £4.5d
rows.notes: This logical entry is the combination of two visual lines (the description continues on the next visual line). The page-specific guidance associates this combined description with the numeric '37 4 5'. There is a minor discrepancy: text indicates interest '4.5d' (half‑penny) but the numeric pence column reads as a '5' character; I followed the numeric column and recorded pence=5. Column alignment was performed using the definitive total row (row 26).
Single '9' in the pounds column; small but legible. Description on its own visual line directly beneath the Kater repayment; column alignment confirmed.
Amount clearly reads '1010 19 8' and is well aligned. Description referencing '2000 Consols' is legible.
Small single-pound entry '1' visible in the pounds column. The name 'Hammond Key' is legible but compact.
Single '5' in the pounds column. Surname spelled 'Allnutt' is readable; spacing is tight but unambiguous.
Pounds '30' visible in the pounds column. Parenthetical '(Choir)' is faint but the entry aligns with preceding small pound amounts.
Final total '6726 11 7' is very clear and boxed at the right. Used as the definitive alignment reference for establishing £/s/d column boundaries for the whole page.
